verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes IP3 receptor type 1, a large tetrameric Ca²⁺ channel exclusively resident in the ER membrane. Its six transmembrane helices are embedded in the ER bilayer; both its large N-terminal ligand-binding domain and its C-terminal regulatory domain face the cytoplasm or ER lumen, not the extracellular space. No PM outer-leaflet pool, no GPI anchor, no shedding, no lysosomal exocytosis biology, no cancer/cell-death/activation-induced surface display, no stable surface TM-partner tethering, and no germline/tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display has been documented for ITPR1. No CD designation; gene-group membership (IP3 receptors) is an endomembrane family. All five contextual buckets are ruled out: (1) no cancer/oncogenic ecto-pool; (2) no cell-death surface exposure; (3) no germline/developmental outer-leaflet display; (4) no activation-induced PM translocation; (5) no wash-resistant TM-partner anchoring at the outer leaflet. No surface-directed therapeutic programs target the ITPR1 protein body from outside the cell.
